AlternativeProduct.description: An alternative splicing product (isoform) of the gene. Corresponds to UniProt isoform entries. Use this to document isoform-specific functions where different isoforms have distinct or even antagonistic biological activities. DEPRECATED: Use FunctionalIsoform instead for curated functional classes.

description.description: Agent-populated description of the isoform's function. Document any isoform-specific functions, expression patterns, or biological activities that differ from other isoforms.

name.description: Common name of the isoform (e.g., Bcl-xL, Bcl-xS)

sequence_note.description: Brief note about sequence differences (e.g., "lacks exon 2", "shorter C-terminus")

AlternativeProduct.title: AlternativeProduct

AnatomicalEntityDescriptor.description: A descriptor for an anatomical entity, tissue, organismal region, or structure.
AnatomicalEntityDescriptor.title: AnatomicalEntityDescriptor

AnnotationQualifierEnum.description: GO annotation qualifiers specifying the relationship between a gene product and a GO term. These correspond to the QUALIFIER column in GAF/GPAD files and the QuickGO API.
AnnotationQualifierEnum.title: AnnotationQualifierEnum

BiologicalProcessDescriptor.description: A descriptor for a biological process, pathway, reaction, or process-like module grounding.
BiologicalProcessDescriptor.title: BiologicalProcessDescriptor

CellTypeDescriptor.description: A descriptor for a cell type or cell state.
CellTypeDescriptor.title: CellTypeDescriptor

CellularComponentDescriptor.description: A descriptor for a cellular component, organelle, compartment, or complex location.
CellularComponentDescriptor.title: CellularComponentDescriptor

ChemicalEntityDescriptor.description: A descriptor for a chemical entity, metabolite, cofactor, ion, or small molecule.
ChemicalEntityDescriptor.title: ChemicalEntityDescriptor

ComplexUnit.description: A role-bearing unit within a protein complex descriptor.

stoichiometry.description: Optional stoichiometry or copy-number statement when known.

ComplexUnit.title: ComplexUnit

ConditionOverlapAssessment.description: Assessment of overlap between rule conditions

notes.description: Notes on condition overlap - e.g., "IPR000001 and IPR000002 both represent the same structural domain" or "FunFam subsumes the InterPro entry"

supported_by.description: Supporting text from literature for this assessment

ConditionOverlapAssessment.title: ConditionOverlapAssessment

ConditionTypeEnum.description: Types of conditions in rule antecedents
ConditionTypeEnum.title: ConditionTypeEnum

CoreFunction.description: A core function is a GO-CAM-like annotation of the core evolved functions of a gene. This is a synthesis of the reviewed core annotations, brought together into a unified GO-CAM-like representation.

contributes_to_molecular_function.description: A molecular function that this gene product contributes to as part of a complex, but does not independently enable. Used for accessory/structural subunits of multi-protein complexes (e.g., an accessory subunit of Complex I contributes_to NADH dehydrogenase activity but does not have that activity on its own). The molecular_function slot should then contain the subunit-specific activity (e.g., structural molecule activity).

in_complex.description: The protein-containing complex (GO:0032991 descendant) that this gene product is an active unit of. Use this — not locations — for complex membership (e.g. ribosome, spliceosome, EMC, signal peptidase complex).

knowledge_gaps.description: Curated, literature-grounded statements of what is NOT known — applicable at the level of the whole gene, a single existing annotation, a core function, a whole module, or a single module step/node. The inverse of core_functions: everywhere else the schema records what IS known; here it records, with the same evidentiary discipline, what is not. See the Function Knowledge Gaps project (projects/FUNCTION_KNOWLEDGE_GAPS.md).

locations.description: Cellular anatomical entities (e.g. membranes, nucleus, cytosol, organelle parts) where the gene product functions. Do NOT use this for protein-containing complexes (GO:0032991 and its descendants) — record complex membership in in_complex instead.

molecular_function.description: The molecular function this gene product enables (i.e., has the activity independently). For complex subunits that contribute to but don't independently have a complex-level activity, use contributes_to_molecular_function instead and put a subunit-specific MF here (e.g., structural constituent of ribosome, electron transfer activity).

CoreFunction.title: CoreFunction

Descriptor.description: A human-friendly descriptor with optional ontology/database grounding. The preferred_term may be more nuanced than the term label, and the term may be absent when no good identifier exists yet.
Descriptor.title: Descriptor

DevelopmentalStageDescriptor.description: A descriptor for a developmental stage, life-cycle stage, or temporal window.
DevelopmentalStageDescriptor.title: DevelopmentalStageDescriptor

DomainDescriptor.description: A descriptor for a protein domain, motif, site, or architectural feature.
DomainDescriptor.title: DomainDescriptor

EmbeddedRule.description: An embedded representation of an ARBA or UniRule for storage in YAML. Captures the essential structure: conditions (antecedent) and GO annotations (consequent).

condition_sets.description: List of condition sets (OR-ed together). Each condition set is a conjunction (AND) of conditions. The rule fires if ANY condition set matches.

entries.description: Entry-centric view of all entities in the rule (domain conditions and GO terms). Each entry tracks its relationships (PREDICTS, PREDICTED_BY, EQUIV) to other entries.

go_annotations.description: GO terms assigned by this rule

ipr2go_redundancy.description: Analysis of redundancy with InterPro2GO mappings

modified_date.description: Date the rule was last modified

reviewed_protein_count.description: Number of reviewed (Swiss-Prot) proteins annotated by this rule

unreviewed_protein_count.description: Number of unreviewed (TrEMBL) proteins annotated by this rule

EmbeddedRule.title: EmbeddedRule

EntryRelationshipEnum.description: Type of relationship between entries in a rule
EntryRelationshipEnum.title: EntryRelationshipEnum

EntryTypeEnum.description: Type of entry in a rule review (domain/family condition or GO term target)
EntryTypeEnum.title: EntryTypeEnum

EvidenceItem.description: A lightweight citable source for module-level assertions. The source may be a PMID, DOI, database record, local file, pathway record, issue, or any other citable artifact. This is deliberately less strict than the publication quote validation used in gene reviews.

source_id.description: Identifier for the evidence source, e.g. PMID:123456, DOI:..., Reactome:R-HSA-..., MetaCyc:..., file:...

statement.description: The assertion this evidence supports in this module.

supporting_text.description: Optional quote or excerpt from the evidence source.

EvidenceItem.title: EvidenceItem

EvidenceType.description: Gene Ontology evidence codes mapped to Evidence and Conclusion Ontology (ECO) terms
EvidenceType.title: EvidenceType

ExistingAnnotation.description: An existing annotation from the GO database, plus a review of the annotation.

evidence_type.description: Evidence code (e.g., IDA, IBA, ISS, TAS)

isoform.description: UniProt isoform identifier (e.g., "P19544-1" for WT1 isoform 1). Only populated when the annotation is specific to a particular isoform rather than the canonical protein sequence. Note that just because an experiment used a particular isoform doesn't mean the annotation is isoform-specific - it may apply to all isoforms. Use this field only when there is clear evidence the annotation is isoform-specific.

qualifier.description: The GO annotation qualifier specifying the relationship between the gene product and the term. For MF annotations, distinguishes 'enables' (gene product has the activity independently) from 'contributes_to' (gene product contributes to a complex's activity but does not have the activity alone). For BP, distinguishes 'involved_in', 'acts_upstream_of', etc. For CC, distinguishes 'located_in', 'part_of', 'is_active_in', 'colocalizes_with'.

retired.description: Whether the annotation is retired or replaced

ExistingAnnotation.title: ExistingAnnotation

Experiment.description: A suggested experiment to answer a question about the gene

description.description: Detailed description of the experiment to be performed

experiment_type.description: Type of experiment or assay to answer the question

Experiment.title: Experiment

FamilyDescriptor.description: A descriptor for a protein family, orthogroup, or other evolutionary grouping.

representative_members.description: Representative concrete members used to orient the family. These are examples, not an exhaustive member list and not a claim that the module is limited to these proteins.

FamilyDescriptor.title: FamilyDescriptor

Finding.description: A finding is a statement about a gene, which is supported by a reference. Similar to "comments" in uniprot

finding_review.description: Manual reviewer assessment of this specific finding - in particular whether the finding remains current, is disputed, or has been overturned/superseded by later evidence. Reviewer-supplied; distinct from the statement/supporting_text that describe the finding itself.

full_text_unavailable.description: Whether the full text is unavailable

reference_section_type.description: Type of section in the reference (e.g., 'ABSTRACT', 'METHODS', 'RESULTS', 'DISCUSSION')

statement.description: Concise statement describing an aspect of the gene

supporting_text.description: Supporting text from the publication. This should be exact substrings. Different substrings can be broken up by '...'s. These substrings will be checked against the actual text of the paper. If editorialization is necessary, put this in square brackets (this is not checked). For example, you can say '...[CFAP300 shows] transport within cilia is IFT dependent...'

Finding.title: Finding

FindingReview.description: Manual reviewer assessment of a specific finding within a reference - in particular whether it remains current, is disputed, or has been overturned/superseded by later evidence. This is finer-grained than reference_review (which assesses the whole reference); a paper may contain some findings that stand and others that are overturned. All fields optional and reviewer-supplied.

finding_status.description: Reviewer's assessment of the empirical standing of a specific finding in light of other evidence (e.g. whether it has been disputed or overturned).

review_notes.description: Free-text note explaining the relevance/correctness judgment (e.g. what was verified, or why a citation is wrong, disputed, or low quality).

superseded_by.description: Reference(s) that dispute, correct, or overturn this finding. Used together with finding_status DISPUTED or OVERTURNED.

FindingReview.title: FindingReview

FindingReviewStatusEnum.description: Reviewer's assessment of the empirical standing of a specific finding (a statement extracted from a reference) in light of other evidence. Unlike ReferenceCorrectnessEnum, which judges a whole reference, this applies per finding: a paper may have some findings that stand and others that have been overturned. Use superseded_by to point to the reference(s) responsible.
FindingReviewStatusEnum.title: FindingReviewStatusEnum

FunctionalIsoform.description: A curated functional isoform class. Unlike AlternativeProduct (which maps 1:1 to UniProt isoforms), this captures FUNCTIONALLY RELEVANT distinctions that may: - Group multiple UniProt isoforms into a functional class (e.g., WT1 +KTS isoforms) - Represent cleavage products from polyproteins (e.g., POMC peptides) - Describe modification states or conformational variants Only create entries when there ARE functionally distinct forms worth documenting.

description.description: Detailed description of this functional class. Document the specific functions, how they differ from other classes, tissue specificity, and any antagonistic relationships (e.g., "OREXIGENIC - opposite to alpha-MSH").

id.description: Curator-defined identifier for this functional class. Use a descriptive format like GENE_CLASS (e.g., WT1_PLUS_KTS, POMC_ACTH, BCL2L1_XL).

isoform_specific_terms.description: GO terms that are specific to this functional class. These are terms that should NOT be annotated to the gene as a whole, only to this specific form. Using Term objects enables linkml-term-validator checking.

maps_to.description: Mappings to underlying UniProt identifiers. Optional - some functional classes may not map cleanly to specific UniProt IDs.

name.description: Human-readable name for this functional class (e.g., "+KTS isoforms", "ACTH/Corticotropin", "Bcl-xL").

type.description: Type of functional distinction (SPLICE_VARIANT, SPLICE_CLASS, CLEAVAGE_PRODUCT, MODIFICATION_STATE, CONFORMATIONAL_STATE).

FunctionalIsoform.title: FunctionalIsoform

FunctionalIsoformMapping.description: A mapping from a functional isoform class to underlying UniProt identifiers. Allows grouping multiple UniProt isoforms or chains into a single functional class.

ids.description: UniProt identifiers belonging to this functional class. For UNIPROT_ISOFORM: P19544-1, P19544-2, etc. For UNIPROT_CHAIN: PRO_0000024969, PRO_0000024970, etc.

residues.description: Residue range for cleavage products (e.g., "138-176" for ACTH). Only applicable for UNIPROT_CHAIN type.

FunctionalIsoformMapping.title: FunctionalIsoformMapping

FunctionalIsoformMappingTypeEnum.description: Type of identifier that a functional isoform maps to
FunctionalIsoformMappingTypeEnum.title: FunctionalIsoformMappingTypeEnum

FunctionalIsoformTypeEnum.description: Type of functional isoform or product. Distinguishes between different mechanisms that produce functionally distinct forms of a gene product.
FunctionalIsoformTypeEnum.title: FunctionalIsoformTypeEnum

GOBiologicalProcessEnum.description: A biological process term in the GO ontology
GOBiologicalProcessEnum.title: GOBiologicalProcessEnum

GOCellularLocationEnum.description: A cellular location term in the GO ontology (excludes protein-containing complexes)
GOCellularLocationEnum.title: GOCellularLocationEnum

GOMolecularActivityEnum.description: A molecular activity term in the GO ontology
GOMolecularActivityEnum.title: GOMolecularActivityEnum

GOProteinContainingComplexEnum.description: A protein-containing complex term in the GO ontology
GOProteinContainingComplexEnum.title: GOProteinContainingComplexEnum

notes.description: Notes on specificity - suggested alternative terms if too broad/narrow

supported_by.description: Supporting text from literature for this assessment

GOTermEnum.description: A term in the GO ontology (including roots, to allow ND annotations)
GOTermEnum.title: GOTermEnum

GeneDescriptor.description: A descriptor for a gene or locus.
GeneDescriptor.title: GeneDescriptor

GeneProductDescriptor.description: A descriptor for a gene product, protein, isoform, or gene-product form.
GeneProductDescriptor.title: GeneProductDescriptor

alternative_products.description: Alternative splicing products (isoforms) of the gene. Seeded from UniProt ALTERNATIVE PRODUCTS section. Only populated if there are multiple isoforms. Use this to document isoform-specific functions and biology. DEPRECATED: Use functional_isoforms instead for curated functional classes.

functional_isoforms.description: Curated functional isoform classes for the gene. Unlike alternative_products (which is seeded from UniProt), this field is purely curator/agent-defined to capture FUNCTIONALLY RELEVANT distinctions. Examples: - Splice classes that group multiple UniProt isoforms (e.g., WT1 +KTS vs -KTS) - Cleavage products from polyproteins (e.g., POMC peptides) - Modification states with distinct functions Only populate when there ARE functionally distinct forms worth documenting.

knowledge_gaps.description: Curated, literature-grounded statements of what is NOT known — applicable at the level of the whole gene, a single existing annotation, a core function, a whole module, or a single module step/node. The inverse of core_functions: everywhere else the schema records what IS known; here it records, with the same evidentiary discipline, what is not. See the Function Knowledge Gaps project (projects/FUNCTION_KNOWLEDGE_GAPS.md).

product_type.description: Type of gene product (protein, ncRNA, etc.)

proposed_new_terms.description: Proposed new ontology terms that should exist but don't

status.description: Overall status of the gene review

suggested_questions.description: Suggested questions to ask experts about the gene. Only include if not obvious from the literature.

tags.description: Tags associated with the gene for categorization and organization

GeneReviewStatusEnum.description: Status of the gene review process
GeneReviewStatusEnum.title: GeneReviewStatusEnum

InterPro2GORedundancy.description: Analysis of whether rule GO annotations are redundant with existing InterPro2GO mappings from the GO Consortium.

novel_annotations.description: GO IDs not found in InterPro2GO for any rule condition

redundant_annotations.description: GO annotations that already exist in InterPro2GO

InterPro2GORedundancy.title: InterPro2GORedundancy

InterProTypeEnum.description: InterPro entry types categorizing protein signatures
InterProTypeEnum.title: InterProTypeEnum

KnowledgeGap.description: A curated, literature-grounded statement of what is NOT known about a gene product, core function, module, or step — its molecular activity, mechanism, partner(s), localization, or biological role. A knowledge gap is a reviewer judgment reached by reading the primary literature, NOT a pattern in the annotations: a heavily annotated gene can hide a gaping mechanistic hole, and a sparsely annotated one can be perfectly understood and merely under-curated. Each gap is a small, defensible scholarly object with the same evidentiary discipline used for positive claims. See projects/FUNCTION_KNOWLEDGE_GAPS.md for the methodology and worked exemplars.

boundary.description: What IS firmly established, so the gap is sharply delimited. The edge of current knowledge against which the gap is defined.

dark_aspect.description: Which GO aspect (or pattern) is dark for this gap. Most "dark" genes are not uniformly dark.

gap_kind.description: The kind(s) of ignorance — biology, curation, and/or ontology — which determines who can resolve it. Multiple values denote a blend (e.g. a biology gap with an ontology shadow).

gap_statement.description: The specific unknown, stated precisely. Not "role unclear" but e.g. "the direct substrate / the catalytic activity / the essential partner is undetermined".

proposed_terms.description: For ONTOLOGY gaps, the new ontology term(s) that would let the knowledge be expressed (e.g. "structural constituent of complex X"). May elaborate the gene's top-level proposed_new_terms.

provenance.description: Evidence that the unknown is REAL and not merely uncurated — ideally the field's own admissions of ignorance ("remains to be determined", "the precise role is unknown"). The supporting_text is a verbatim substring of the cited reference and is checked by the reference validator exactly like supported_by. When the only available source is a DOI-only paper or a local analysis, anchor provenance to that reference_id (e.g. a file: path).

resolution.description: What would resolve the gap — the experiment, ontology term, or curation action. For ONTOLOGY gaps, pair with proposed_terms (or the gene's top-level proposed_new_terms).

status.description: Lifecycle status of the gap, tracking progress toward resolution.

KnowledgeGap.title: KnowledgeGap

KnowledgeGapAspectEnum.description: Which GO aspect (or pattern) is dark for a knowledge gap. Most "dark" genes are not uniformly dark.
KnowledgeGapAspectEnum.title: KnowledgeGapAspectEnum

KnowledgeGapKindEnum.description: The kind of ignorance a knowledge gap represents, which determines who can resolve it. A single gap may carry several values when it is a blend.
KnowledgeGapKindEnum.title: KnowledgeGapKindEnum

KnowledgeGapStatusEnum.description: Lifecycle status of a knowledge gap, tracking progress toward resolution.
KnowledgeGapStatusEnum.title: KnowledgeGapStatusEnum

LiteratureSupportAssessment.description: Assessment of literature support for the rule

notes.description: Notes on literature support - key papers, gaps in evidence

supported_by.description: Supporting text from literature for this assessment

LiteratureSupportAssessment.title: LiteratureSupportAssessment

LiteratureSupportEnum.description: Level of literature support for the rule
LiteratureSupportEnum.title: LiteratureSupportEnum

ManuscriptSection.description: Sections of a scientific manuscript or publication
ManuscriptSection.title: ManuscriptSection

ModuleAnnoton.description: A leaf role assertion in a module. The participant may be a concrete gene or an abstract selector, and the function/process/location fields are descriptor holders rather than direct GO annotation exports.
ModuleAnnoton.title: ModuleAnnoton

ModuleConnection.description: A connection between module nodes, annotons, or other named elements. Source and target are IDs scoped to the module document.
ModuleConnection.title: ModuleConnection

ModuleConnectionTypeEnum.description: Common connection types between module elements.
ModuleConnectionTypeEnum.title: ModuleConnectionTypeEnum

ModuleContext.description: Context that applies to a module node, variant, annoton, or connection.
ModuleContext.title: ModuleContext

ModuleNode.description: A node in a module. Nodes can be recursively decomposed using parts and variant_sets, and may also carry leaf annotons and connections.

concepts.description: Optional ontology/database grounding for this module node.

knowledge_gaps.description: Curated, literature-grounded statements of what is NOT known — applicable at the level of the whole gene, a single existing annotation, a core function, a whole module, or a single module step/node. The inverse of core_functions: everywhere else the schema records what IS known; here it records, with the same evidentiary discipline, what is not. See the Function Knowledge Gaps project (projects/FUNCTION_KNOWLEDGE_GAPS.md).

ModuleNode.title: ModuleNode

ModulePart.description: A conjunctive part or step of a module node.

optional.description: Whether this part is optional in the parent module.

order.description: Optional display or temporal order. Equal or absent values imply partial ordering only.

role.description: Curator-supplied role of this part within the parent module.

ModulePart.title: ModulePart

ModuleReview.description: Review or curation record for a recursively decomposable biological module. This can describe a pathway, organelle lifecycle, protein complex, molecular function, developmental process, or abstract/evolutionary functional plan.

knowledge_gaps.description: Curated, literature-grounded statements of what is NOT known — applicable at the level of the whole gene, a single existing annotation, a core function, a whole module, or a single module step/node. The inverse of core_functions: everywhere else the schema records what IS known; here it records, with the same evidentiary discipline, what is not. See the Function Knowledge Gaps project (projects/FUNCTION_KNOWLEDGE_GAPS.md).

ModuleReview.title: ModuleReview

ModuleTypeEnum.description: Broad type of biological module node.
ModuleTypeEnum.title: ModuleTypeEnum

ModuleVariantSet.description: A set of alternative implementations for a module node or part. Variants may themselves contain parts, annotons, connections, and nested variant sets.

axis.description: The dimension along which these variants differ, e.g. taxon, cell type, compartment, route, enzyme family.

selection.description: How many variants may be selected in a concrete realization.

ModuleVariantSet.title: ModuleVariantSet

MolecularFunctionDescriptor.description: A descriptor for a molecular function. Extra slots capture common functional nuance without requiring formal post-composition.
MolecularFunctionDescriptor.title: MolecularFunctionDescriptor

OverlapInterpretationEnum.description: Automated interpretation of domain overlap patterns
OverlapInterpretationEnum.title: OverlapInterpretationEnum

PairwiseOverlap.description: Overlap statistics between two domain conditions (InterPro, FunFam, etc.) in the same condition set. Provides set difference metrics to measure uniqueness and redundancy.

a_minus_b_count.description: Number of proteins in A but not in B (|A - B|). Represents the uniqueness of A with respect to B. High value = A adds unique coverage beyond B. Zero value = A is completely contained in B (A ⊆ B).

b_minus_a_count.description: Number of proteins in B but not in A (|B - A|). Represents the uniqueness of B with respect to A. High value = B adds unique coverage beyond A. Zero value = B is completely contained in A (B ⊆ A).

condition_a_in_sets.description: List of 1-based condition set indices where condition A appears

condition_b_in_sets.description: List of 1-based condition set indices where condition B appears

containment_a_in_b.description: Proportion of A contained in B: |A ∩ B| / |A|. 1.0 means A is completely contained in B (A ⊆ B).

containment_b_in_a.description: Proportion of B contained in A: |A ∩ B| / |B|. 1.0 means B is completely contained in A (B ⊆ A).

count_a.description: Number of proteins matching condition A in specified database

count_b.description: Number of proteins matching condition B in specified database

intersection_count.description: Number of proteins matching BOTH A AND B (|A ∩ B|) in specified database

jaccard_similarity.description: Jaccard similarity coefficient: |A ∩ B| / |A ∪ B| = intersection / (count_a + count_b - intersection). 0.0 = no overlap, 1.0 = complete overlap.

protein_database.description: Which protein database was queried (SWISSPROT or TREMBL)

PairwiseOverlap.title: PairwiseOverlap

ParsimonyAssessment.description: Assessment of rule parsimony (simplicity vs complexity)

notes.description: Notes on parsimony - e.g., which conditions are redundant

supported_by.description: Supporting text from literature for this assessment

ParsimonyAssessment.title: ParsimonyAssessment

ParsimonyEnum.description: Assessment of rule parsimony (simplicity vs complexity)
ParsimonyEnum.title: ParsimonyEnum

ParticipantSelector.description: A selector for a concrete or abstract participant in a module annoton. This can ground to a gene, gene product, complex, family, domain, ortholog, homolog, or any entity satisfying a functional/domain constraint.
ParticipantSelector.title: ParticipantSelector

ParticipantSelectorTypeEnum.description: How a module annoton participant is selected.
ParticipantSelectorTypeEnum.title: ParticipantSelectorTypeEnum

PredictedAnnotation.description: A single computational prediction and its review. Each prediction comes from a specific method and predicts a term (EC number, GO term, etc.) for the gene.

predicted_term.description: The predicted term (GO term, EC number, etc.)

predicted_term_type.description: Type of predicted term (EC, GO_MF, GO_BP, GO_CC)

source_method.description: Name of the prediction method (e.g., DeepECTF, PANTHER_IBA, InterPro2GO, CLEAN, GloEC, MAPred, ProteinInfer)

source_reference_id.description: Reference for the prediction method or study (e.g., PMID:37820725 for the Kim et al. 2023 DeepECTF study)

source_version.description: Version or date of the prediction method

PredictedAnnotation.title: PredictedAnnotation

PredictionAssessment.description: Assessment of a single computational prediction. Uses categories from de Crécy-Lagard et al. 2025 (PMID:40703034) plus extensions for GO predictions.

confidence_score.description: Confidence score following de Crécy-Lagard et al. 2025: 2 = concordant with evidence, 1 = uncertain, 0 = discordant with evidence

error_type.description: Type of error that led to the incorrect prediction, following Table 1 of de Crécy-Lagard et al. 2025

PredictionAssessment.title: PredictionAssessment

PredictionAssessmentEnum.description: Assessment categories for computational predictions, based on de Crécy-Lagard et al. 2025 (PMID:40703034) Fig. 4.
PredictionAssessmentEnum.title: PredictionAssessmentEnum

PredictionErrorTypeEnum.description: Types of errors that lead to incorrect functional predictions, based on Table 1 of de Crécy-Lagard et al. 2025 (PMID:40703034).
PredictionErrorTypeEnum.title: PredictionErrorTypeEnum

PredictionReview.description: Review of computational/ML predictions for a gene that are NOT in the curated GOA/UniProt annotations. This captures predictions from methods like DeepECTF, PANTHER/IBA, InterPro2GO, CLEAN, GloEC, MAPred, etc. and evaluates them against literature and bioinformatic evidence. Inspired by the systematic evaluation in de Crécy-Lagard et al. 2025 (PMID:40703034).

description.description: Summary of the prediction review findings

id.description: UniProt accession for the gene product

locus_tag.description: Locus tag for the gene (e.g., b1267 for E. coli)

source_documents.description: Paths to supporting source documents (e.g., reasoning traces, raw model outputs) for provenance

status.description: Overall status of the gene review

PredictionReview.title: PredictionReview

ProposedOntologyTerm.description: A proposed new ontology term that should exist but doesn't currently

justification.description: Justification for why this term is needed

proposed_definition.description: Proposed definition for the new term

proposed_mappings.description: Proposed mappings to equivalent terms in other ontologies

proposed_name.description: Proposed name for the new term

proposed_parent.description: Proposed parent term in the ontology hierarchy

ProposedOntologyTerm.title: ProposedOntologyTerm

ProteinComplexDescriptor.description: A descriptor for a protein-containing complex or subcomplex.

active_units.description: Active units or role-bearing components of the complex. This is used to avoid leaving functionally important complex structure as prose.

ProteinComplexDescriptor.title: ProteinComplexDescriptor

ProteinDatabaseEnum.description: Protein database types for rule analysis
ProteinDatabaseEnum.title: ProteinDatabaseEnum

PublicationTypeEnum.description: The kind of publication or source a reference is. For PMIDs this is inferred from the PubMed publication-type ('PT') metadata; for non-literature references it is inferred from the identifier scheme. Used to test hypotheses about which evidence sources (primary papers, reviews, abstracts, deep research) suffice for GO annotation review.
PublicationTypeEnum.title: PublicationTypeEnum

Question.description: A question to be answered about the gene

experts.description: Experts to answer the question. These should be drawn from the authors of relevant publications already referenced. If no suitable experts are available, it's OK to leave this as an empty list!

Question.title: Question

ROTermEnum.description: A term in the relation ontology
ROTermEnum.title: ROTermEnum

RedundantAnnotation.description: A GO annotation that is redundant with an existing InterPro2GO mapping

interpro_source.description: InterPro ID that already maps to this GO term in ipr2go

RedundantAnnotation.title: RedundantAnnotation

Reference.description: A reference is a published text  that describes a finding or a method. References might be formal publications (where the ID is a PMID), or for methods, a GO_REF. Additionally, a reference to a local ad-hoc analysis or review can be made by using the `file:` prefix.

full_text_unavailable.description: Whether the full text is unavailable

is_invalid.description: Whether the reference is invalid (e.g., retracted or replaced)

publication_type.description: The kind of publication or source this reference is (e.g. primary research article, review, meta-analysis, database record, AI deep-research report). For PMIDs this is normally inferred from the PubMed publication-type ('PT') metadata rather than set by hand; for non-PMID references (GO_REF, Reactome, file:) it is inferred from the identifier. Lets analyses ask, e.g., whether review articles or abstracts alone are sufficient to support a given annotation action.

reference_review.description: Manual reviewer assessment of this reference (relevance, and citation correctness / scientific soundness). Reviewer-supplied, distinct from the machine-fetched id/title.

Reference.title: Reference

ReferenceCorrectnessEnum.description: Reviewer's overall manual assessment of a reference's trustworthiness, spanning both citation correctness (does the identifier point to the intended paper that supports its use) and scientific soundness (is that paper's claim reliable). Single-valued: record the most salient issue and elaborate in review_notes. Complements is_invalid (retracted/replaced) and full_text_unavailable.
ReferenceCorrectnessEnum.title: ReferenceCorrectnessEnum

ReferenceRelevanceEnum.description: Reviewer's assessment of how relevant a reference is to the gene's function and review.
ReferenceRelevanceEnum.title: ReferenceRelevanceEnum

ReferenceReview.description: Manual reviewer assessment of a reference - how relevant it is to the gene's function, and whether it is correctly cited and scientifically sound. Distinct from the machine-fetched id/title fields; all fields are optional and reviewer-supplied.

correctness.description: Reviewer's overall assessment of a reference's trustworthiness - both citation correctness (the identifier resolves to the intended paper that supports its use) and scientific soundness of that paper's claim.

relevance.description: Reviewer judgment of how relevant the reference is to the gene's function and this review.

review_notes.description: Free-text note explaining the relevance/correctness judgment (e.g. what was verified, or why a citation is wrong, disputed, or low quality).

ReferenceReview.title: ReferenceReview

RelatedEntry.description: A relationship from this entry to another entry in the rule. Categorized as PREDICTS (this → other), PREDICTED_BY (other → this), or EQUIV (bidirectional).

containment.description: Containment score (0-1) for the directional relationship. For PREDICTS: this_in_target (how much of this is contained in target). For PREDICTED_BY: target_in_this (how much of target is contained in this). For EQUIV: max of both directions.

exclusive_count.description: Number of proteins exclusive to the "source" of the relationship. For PREDICTS: proteins in this but not target. For PREDICTED_BY: proteins in target but not this. For EQUIV: proteins in this but not target (A - B).

intersection_count.description: Number of proteins in both this and target

RelatedEntry.title: RelatedEntry

RelationDescriptor.description: A descriptor for a relation or connection predicate.
RelationDescriptor.title: RelationDescriptor

Review.description: A review of an existing annotation.

knowledge_gaps.description: Curated, literature-grounded statements of what is NOT known — applicable at the level of the whole gene, a single existing annotation, a core function, a whole module, or a single module step/node. The inverse of core_functions: everywhere else the schema records what IS known; here it records, with the same evidentiary discipline, what is not. See the Function Knowledge Gaps project (projects/FUNCTION_KNOWLEDGE_GAPS.md).

proposed_replacement_terms.description: If the action is MODIFY, then this is a list of proposed replacement terms

Review.title: Review

RuleCondition.description: A single condition in a rule antecedent

interpro_type.description: InterPro entry type (family, domain, active_site, etc.). Only populated for InterPro conditions (condition_type = INTERPRO). Extracted from InterPro metadata or API.

negated.description: Whether this is a negative condition (NOT)

protein_count.description: Number of proteins matching this condition in specified database. Only populated for domain/family conditions (InterPro, FunFam, PANTHER). Null for taxon and other condition types.

protein_database.description: Which protein database was queried (e.g., SWISSPROT, TREMBL). Defaults to SWISSPROT (reviewed proteins only). Important to specify since counts differ dramatically between databases.

sample_proteins.description: Sample UniProt IDs matching this condition. Only included when protein_count < 20 to avoid bloating files. Limited to max 10 examples.

uniqueness_score.description: Measure of domain uniqueness (0.0 to 1.0). Calculated as 1.0 - mean(containment in other domains in same condition set). High score = more unique/specific domain. Low score = broad domain that commonly co-occurs.

RuleCondition.title: RuleCondition

RuleConditionSet.description: A set of conditions that must ALL be true (conjunction/AND). Multiple condition sets in a rule are OR-ed together (disjunction).

conditions.description: Conditions in this set (all must match)

notes.description: Reviewer notes on this specific condition set

number.description: 1-based condition set number (CS1, CS2, CS3, etc.)

pairwise_overlap.description: Pairwise overlap statistics for domain conditions in this set. Only computed for InterPro, FunFam, PANTHER conditions. Provides set difference metrics (uniqueness) and Jaccard similarity.

RuleConditionSet.title: RuleConditionSet

RuleGOAnnotation.description: A GO annotation produced by the rule
RuleGOAnnotation.title: RuleGOAnnotation

RuleReview.description: A review of a UniProt annotation rule (ARBA or UniRule). Each review covers ONE rule and assesses its quality, literature support, and biological appropriateness.

condition_overlap.description: Assessment of overlap between rule conditions

confidence.description: Overall confidence in the rule (0.0 to 1.0)

literature_support.description: Assessment of literature support for the rule

parsimony.description: Assessment of rule parsimony (simplicity vs complexity)

review_summary.description: Overall summary of the review findings

suggested_modifications.description: Specific modifications suggested if action is MODIFY

supported_by.description: Supporting text from literature for this review

RuleReview.title: RuleReview

RuleReviewEntry.description: An entity in the rule - either a domain/family condition or a GO term target. Each entry tracks its relationships (predictions, predicted-by, equivalence) to other entries.

appears_in_condition_sets.description: Which condition sets (1-based) contain this entry (for domain conditions only)

asserted_predicted_go_terms.description: GO terms that this entry maps to via external mappings (e.g., ipr2go). Only populated for external entries not in the rule's condition sets.

protein_count.description: Number of proteins matching this condition (from SwissProt)

related_entries.description: Relationships to other entries in the rule

source.description: Source of this entry if external to the rule (e.g., 'ipr2go' for InterPro entries that map to the same GO term via InterPro2GO but are not part of any condition set)

type.description: Type of entry (INTERPRO, FUNFAM, PANTHER, GO_TERM, etc.)

RuleReviewEntry.title: RuleReviewEntry

SupportingTextInReference.description: A supporting text in a reference.

full_text_unavailable.description: Whether the full text is unavailable

reference_section_type.description: Type of section in the reference (e.g., 'ABSTRACT', 'METHODS', 'RESULTS', 'DISCUSSION')

supporting_text.description: Supporting text from the publication. This should be exact substrings. Different substrings can be broken up by '...'s. These substrings will be checked against the actual text of the paper. If editorialization is necessary, put this in square brackets (this is not checked). For example, you can say '...[CFAP300 shows] transport within cilia is IFT dependent...'

supporting_text_fulltext.description: Supporting text from the full-text PDF when the full text cannot be committed to the repository. This is an interim solution for cases where we have access to full text but cannot share it publicly. Unlike supporting_text, this field is not validated against cached publication text.

SupportingTextInReference.title: SupportingTextInReference

TaxonDescriptor.description: A descriptor for a taxon or taxonomic scope.
TaxonDescriptor.title: TaxonDescriptor

notes.description: Notes on taxonomic scope - suggested changes to taxon constraints

supported_by.description: Supporting text from literature for this assessment

Term.description: A term in a specific ontology

id.description: A CURIE for a term or database object in GO, CL, CHEBI, UniProtKB, PANTHER, etc.

Term.title: Term

TermMapping.description: A mapping between the proposed term and an equivalent term in another ontology

target_term.description: The target term in another ontology

TermMapping.title: TermMapping

VariantSelectionEnum.description: How variants in a variant set may be selected in a realization.
VariantSelectionEnum.title: VariantSelectionEnum

alternative_products.description: Alternative splicing products (isoforms) of the gene. Seeded from UniProt ALTERNATIVE PRODUCTS section. Only populated if there are multiple isoforms. Use this to document isoform-specific functions and biology. DEPRECATED: Use functional_isoforms instead for curated functional classes.

functional_isoforms.description: Curated functional isoform classes for the gene. Unlike alternative_products (which is seeded from UniProt), this field is purely curator/agent-defined to capture FUNCTIONALLY RELEVANT distinctions. Examples: - Splice classes that group multiple UniProt isoforms (e.g., WT1 +KTS vs -KTS) - Cleavage products from polyproteins (e.g., POMC peptides) - Modification states with distinct functions Only populate when there ARE functionally distinct forms worth documenting.

knowledge_gaps.description: Curated, literature-grounded statements of what is NOT known — applicable at the level of the whole gene, a single existing annotation, a core function, a whole module, or a single module step/node. The inverse of core_functions: everywhere else the schema records what IS known; here it records, with the same evidentiary discipline, what is not. See the Function Knowledge Gaps project (projects/FUNCTION_KNOWLEDGE_GAPS.md).

product_type.description: Type of gene product (protein, ncRNA, etc.)

proposed_new_terms.description: Proposed new ontology terms that should exist but don't

status.description: Overall status of the gene review

suggested_questions.description: Suggested questions to ask experts about the gene. Only include if not obvious from the literature.

tags.description: Tags associated with the gene for categorization and organization
